name: ExtensionDiagnosticReport_Composition
title: R5: Reference to a Composition resource for the DiagnosticReport structure (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DiagnosticReport.composition` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DiagnosticReport.composition` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Composition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `DiagnosticReport.composition` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Composition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DiagnosticReport.composition` has a context of DiagnosticReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DiagnosticReport`.
Element `DiagnosticReport.composition` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Reference to a Composition resource for the DiagnosticReport structure (new)
element.comment:
Element `DiagnosticReport.composition` has a context of DiagnosticReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DiagnosticReport`.
Element `DiagnosticReport.composition` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The Composition provides structure to the content of the DiagnosticReport (and only contains contents referenced in the DiagnosticReport) - e.g., to order the sections of an anatomic pathology structured report.

element.short: Reference to a Composition resource for the DiagnosticReport structure
element.definition: Reference to a Composition resource instance that provides structure for organizing the contents of the DiagnosticReport.
element.comment: The Composition provides structure to the content of the DiagnosticReport (and only contains contents referenced in the DiagnosticReport) - e.g., to order the sections of an anatomic pathology structured report.
